title: cuphea bracteolosa
intro: Cuphea bracteolosa, also known as the Mexican heather or false heather, is a flowering plant native to Mexico and Central America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory, making it effective in treating wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The plant's health benefits include its potential to reduce stress and anxiety, improve sleep quality, and boost the immune system. Cuphea bracteolosa can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a versatile herb for various herbal preparations.
